_comment: Platform-scoped manifest for the marathon product. Discovered by mcp-host.ts alongside claude.md + .claude.json. Defines hot memory + per-kind task handlers (preScript + prompt template).

process_activity.prompt:
A new Strava activity was just stored as a `marathon:strava-activity` Beads issue. Task ID: {{taskId}}. Webhook payload: {{payloadJson}}.

Hot memory snapshot (open plan, recent workouts, effort logs, activities):
{{hotMemoryJson}}

Evaluate this activity against this week's planned workouts. If the agent's heuristics suggest overreach (see claude.md for thresholds), call `flag_overtraining`. If tomorrow's workout needs adjustment, call `update_workout`. If you need older context, call `query_history`. Use `get_plan` first if hot memory is missing the plan.

log_effort.prompt:
A perceived-effort log was just stored as a `marathon:effort-log` Beads issue. Task ID: {{taskId}}. Payload: {{payloadJson}}.

Hot memory snapshot:
{{hotMemoryJson}}

If the score is concerning (RPE ≥ 8 on what was meant to be an easy session, or sustained high RPE across the recent window), follow the heuristics in claude.md and either `flag_overtraining` or `update_workout` to back off the next session. If everything is in band, finish without writes.

create_plan.prompt:
A new race and plan shell were just created. Task ID: {{taskId}}. Payload: {{payloadJson}}. Hot memory: {{hotMemoryJson}}.

This task has no agent work — the shim already enqueued backfill_strava and generate_plan as follow-on tasks. Exit silently without writes.

backfill_strava.prompt:
Historical Strava activities were just imported. Task ID: {{taskId}}. Payload: {{payloadJson}}. Hot memory: {{hotMemoryJson}}.

No agent action required — generate_plan will see these activities in its hot memory. Exit silently.

generate_plan.prompt:
A plan shell exists with a race, training-plan record, and plan-context. Task ID: {{taskId}}. Payload: {{payloadJson}}. Hot memory (race, plan, context, recent Strava activity from backfill): {{hotMemoryJson}}.

Your job: read the plan-context notes, read the available templates via list_templates and read_template, choose the closest fit, then build out the full plan using create_week_block and create_workout. Honor every rule in claude.md (template selection, mileage cap, taper structure, partner/injury constraints). When done, you may also call update_plan_meta to set goalPace if the user implied one. Make no flag_overtraining calls in this task — that is for runtime adjustments only.
